Cluster of black walnuts right next to the trail between the Hyatt and Olentangy river. Many of the fruit has rotted by now but can be harvested for seed. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: September-October.

Ripe black walnut can be collected from the sidewalk. 

If they’re ready, you should be able to easily stomp/roll the outer husk off to get to the nut. Don’t be deterred if you see worms! They make the husk soft enough to remove. 

Commons Ecology / OSU / AU25
